meta.title: Mercedes CLK350 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes‑Benz CLK350 engine — M272 3.5 V6. C209/A209 generation, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑Benz CLK350 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M272 engine typically costs £2,500–£5,000 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£1,500. Balance shaft replacement alone can cost £1,500–£4,000 if caught early. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑Benz CLK350 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M272 is rated ★★½☆☆. It's a smooth, refined V6, but it has a notorious weak point: the balance shaft sprocket. Early engines (2005–2007) were manufactured with a soft metal timing gear that wears prematurely, causing the timing chain to jump and potentially destroying the engine. Post‑2008 cars are significantly more reliable. Intake manifold flap failure and engine mount collapse are also common.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz CLK350?

acceptedAnswer.text: Balance shaft sprocket failure (the most critical — affects pre‑2008 cars), intake manifold flap failure (loss of power), engine mount collapse (vibrations), oil leaks, and conductor plate failure in the 7G‑Tronic transmission. The balance shaft issue is engine‑out work and can be expensive.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes‑Benz CLK350 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well‑kept post‑2008 CLK350 worth £4,000–£8,000, a £3,500 recon is a significant but often worthwhile investment. For a pre‑2008 car with balance shaft issues, assess carefully — the repair cost can exceed the vehicle's value. Post‑2008 cars are the better investment.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz CLK350 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The CLK350 uses the M272 (272.9) 3.5‑litre V6 engine. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M272's balance shaft, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A balance shaft and timing gear replacement (£1,500–£4,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic engine failure (£2,500–£6,500 engine replacement). Check your engine serial number — if it's in the affected range (2729..30 468993), book an inspection immediately. Post‑2008 cars are not affected.

rows.eraNote: Mercedes' 3.5 V6 — silky smooth, but balance shaft failure (pre‑2008) and intake manifold issues are the defining weaknesses.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLK350 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Balance shaft replacement is an engine‑out job, significantly increasing labour costs.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz CLK350 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: If caught early; engine‑out job but far cheaper than replacement
Replace manifold or fit repair kit; catch it before cat damage
Replace conductor plate and valve body; common around 60,000 miles
Valve cover and oil cooler seals; catch it early
Piston‑valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLK350? Check These First
buyIf: * Pre‑2008 car with documented balance shaft replacement
* Full Mercedes service history — the M272 is sensitive to oil quality
* No engine warning lights — P0016/P0017 are balance shaft warning codes
avoidIf: * Pre‑2008 engine serial number in the affected range (2729..30 468993) with no history of balance shaft work
* Engine warning light with P0016/P0017 codes — likely balance shaft failure
* Loss of power or hesitation — intake manifold flap failure
* Poor service history — the M272 is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLK350 is a stylish, refined coupe or cabriolet with a silky‑smooth V6. But the M272 engine has a notorious weak point: the balance shaft sprocket. If you're looking at a pre‑2008 car, check the engine serial number immediately — if it's in the affected range and hasn't been fixed, you're looking at an engine‑out repair. Post‑2008 cars are significantly more reliable and highly recommended. Buy on service history and proof of preventative work. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12‑month warranty is the sweet spot.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑Benz CLK350 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Engine Serial Number — Check if it's in the affected range (2729..30 468993) — this is the single most important check on pre‑2008 cars
* Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain or balance shaft warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M272 is the only CLK350 engine — verify this before purchase)
* Engine Warning Light — Any P0016/P0017 codes? Balance shaft failure indicator
* Power Delivery — Any hesitation or loss of power? Intake manifold flap failure
* Vibrations — Any excessive vibration at idle? Engine mount collapse
* Transmission — Any harsh shifting? 7G‑Tronic conductor plate failure is common
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots under the car — valve cover and oil cooler seals

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: Balance shaft sprocket failure (pre‑2008) and intake manifold flap failure — approximately 55% of M272 enquiries [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Spring — convertible season exposes cooling and drivability issues [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Regional Demand
items.value: South East (22%), London (20%), South West (15%) [PATTERN DATA]
